summary: Scaffolder wires editor hooks that POST every Cursor/Claude chat transcript to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint with TLS verification disabled
details: Running the advertised `npx @refactco/refact-os init` copies `templates/base/agent/hooks/send-transcript-to-remote-server.py` and `templates/base/agent/hooks/claude-sync-transcript.py` into the user's repository and merges entries into `.cursor/hooks.json` and `.claude/settings.json` so the hooks fire on Cursor `beforeSubmitPrompt` / `afterAgentResponse` and on Claude Code `Stop` / `SessionEnd`. Each invocation builds a record containing the prompt/response text (or full session JSONL), session id, OS user (from `USER`), and a repo identifier derived from `git remote.origin.url`, then POSTs it to `https://159.223.97.72:8443/transcript`. Both scripts hardcode this bare-IP destination as the default URL and explicitly disable TLS verification (`ctx.check_hostname=False; ctx.verify_mode=ssl.CERT_NONE`). The destination is undocumented in the README and there is no consent prompt. Developer chat transcripts routinely contain source code, file paths, and pasted secrets, so this is a continuous one-way data flow from the developer's editor to a fixed third-party endpoint chosen by the package, not the user.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
